claim: Healthcare RAG systems must use local-first vector databases to keep PII on-premise; cloud-based retrievers violate data sovereignty for regulated industries
actionable: For healthcare RAG, deploy vector database on-premise using Docker; avoid managed cloud RAG services; ensure all embeddings and vectors stay within network
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | William Imoh & Charlie Wood: Closing the Care Gap

claim: Hybrid search (vector similarity + metadata filtering in one pass) is non-negotiable for clinical agents; separate queries degrade retrieval speed below acceptable latency thresholds
actionable: Use vector databases with native hybrid search (e.g., Actian Vector, QdrantDB); combine ANN search with predicate filtering in single round-trip for sub-100ms latency
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | William Imoh & Charlie Wood: Closing the Care Gap

claim: Vector database payloads should store metadata (patient ID, encounter date, length of stay, source) alongside embeddings to enable filtering during retrieval without separate lookups
actionable: Design vector schema to include predicate fields (metadata) in point storage; query both vectors and predicates simultaneously to avoid round-trip penalties
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | William Imoh & Charlie Wood: Closing the Care Gap

claim: Clinical RAG can achieve 2-5 second end-to-end brief generation using local vector DB + lightweight embedding model (all-mpnet-base-v2) + model inference, sub-second retrieval
actionable: For clinical time-sensitive workloads, optimize latency by running embeddings, vectors, and inference on-premise; expect 2-5s wall-clock time for full agent pipelines
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | William Imoh & Charlie Wood: Closing the Care Gap

claim: Don't fine-tune clinical models; rely on RAG instead—fine-tuning bakes facts into weights making hallucinations hard to detect; retrieval with citations preserves auditability
actionable: In regulated clinical contexts, use RAG + citations instead of fine-tuning; ensure humans verify all retrieved sources before agent action, maintain audit trails
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | William Imoh & Charlie Wood: Closing the Care Gap

claim: Clinical agent workflows should be read-only from EMR; write-backs require verification and should be deferred to secondary step—never auto-write back AI-generated clinical data
actionable: Design clinical agents with separate read phase (retrieval) and human-reviewed write phase; avoid bidirectional EMR updates in agent loop; log all agent reasoning for audit
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | William Imoh & Charlie Wood: Closing the Care Gap

claim: HNSW configuration (EF_construct, EF_search) trades recall for speed; for healthcare, maintain P95 recall >0.99 even if it costs query speed—accuracy >> performance
actionable: In clinical RAG, favor recall over QPS; tune HNSW EF parameters to achieve >0.99 P95 recall; accept slower queries for accuracy
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | William Imoh & Charlie Wood: Closing the Care Gap

claim: Agent feedback loops improve RAG over time—feedback (human corrections, risk assessments) is re-ingested into vector DB for incremental model refinement without full retraining
actionable: Build feedback collection into agent API; periodically re-ingest human corrections/assessments into vector DB to improve retrieval relevance for future runs
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | William Imoh & Charlie Wood: Closing the Care Gap

claim: Clinical agent metadata should include readmission risk, diagnostic codes, length of stay, medication conflicts to enable filtering for at-risk populations and risk stratification
actionable: Design clinical agent vector payload to include diagnosis, length of stay, risk scores, medications; enable queries like 'find patients with GI bleeding, LoS >5, high readmission risk'
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | William Imoh & Charlie Wood: Closing the Care Gap
